title: GPBoost mixed-effects model for driver and circuit heterogeneity
summary: The dataset has very high-cardinality `Driver` plus repeated race structure.
rationale: The dataset has very high-cardinality `Driver` plus repeated race structure. GPBoost is explicitly built to combine boosted trees with grouped random effects, which is a strong fit for driver-specific and race-specific baseline pit propensity that plain GBDTs may only memorize crudely.
implementation_hint: Train a GPBoost binary model with fixed-effect tabular features and grouped random intercepts for `Driver`, `Race_Year`, and possibly `Driver x Compound` or random coefficients on `TyreLife` by driver. Use it as a standalone specialist or OOF meta-feature for the existing booster.
expected_effect: Improves generalization on rare or shifted driver/race combinations by shrinking entity effects instead of overfitting one-hot/frequency signals.
risk: Operational complexity is higher than standard LightGBM/XGBoost, and gains may be modest if split strategy already captures most entity variation.
